title: azolla pinnata subsp. pinnata
intro: Azolla pinnata subsp. pinnata is a submerged aquatic fern that belongs to the family Azollaceae, commonly known as the mosquito fern or duckweed fern due to its floating leaf structure. The plant has been found to possess various medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant activities. The health benefits of Azolla pinnata subsp. pinnata include the potential treatment of diabetes, hypertension, and certain types of cancer, as well as its role in reducing cholesterol levels and improving kidney function. Herbal preparations of Azolla pinnata subsp. pinnata include infusions, decoctions, and capsules, which are traditionally used in traditional medicine practices in various parts of the world.
